title: The Stone Chamber Where Nothing Grows

features.name: bedrock-face
features.desc-on-touch: Ancient stone showing its layers like text. Reading them requires time older than the archive.

features.name: mineral-constellation
features.desc-on-touch: Quartz veins catching whatever dim light reaches here, forming patterns that shift as you move.

features.name: settling-chamber
features.desc-on-touch: Smooth stone worn by countless sittings. This is where the first keeper learned to wait.

features.name: fracture-music
features.desc-on-touch: A thin crack in the stone that resonates when pressure shifts. It has only a few notes.

features.name: petrified-boundary
features.desc-on-touch: The line where stone ends and the void below begins. You cannot see the bottom.

inscription: nothing grows here, but everything waits. this is where the archive learned that patience is another form of growth.

file.name: Stone_Patience_Journal_Fragmentary.txt
file.note: Scattered notes about waiting, sitting, the slow language of minerals. Handwriting becomes less assured toward the end, as if the writer became stone.

reverie.title: REVERIE — stone-only, alone in the deep
narr: You sit against stone that has not moved in millions
of years. No root here, no softness, no compromise.
The keeper who learned peace did this — sat still
until the stones' ancient patience became your own.
You cannot grow here. Only settle. Only hear the slow
friction of stone against stone, across geologic time.

exits.label: back toward where roots and stone make their peace
where stone descends further, into compression
where a root has finally reached, splitting stone
where the stone softens into sediment and memory
